title: Urgent Mitigation: Pre-emptive Buffer Stock for SEMI-MCU-32
situation: The Asia-Europe (Suez) shipping lane is currently DISRUPTED due to a high-severity incident. Single-source supplier SUP-001 (SemiTech Asia) is directly impacted, placing critical semiconductor supply (SEMI-MCU-32) at risk. Revenue at risk: $1,152,000; SLA penalties: $680,000.
recommendation: APPROVE — Pre-emptive Buffer Stock Build of 6,400 units to secure supply runway. If shipping delays exceed 14 days, initiate secondary plan: Emergency Airfreight.

auditLog.text: Disruption in Asia-Europe (Suez) lane threatens $1.8M in financial exposure. Current inventory for SEMI-MCU-32 is 12 days, well below the 30-day target buffer. Pre-emptive buffer build mitigates SLA breach risk for BMW and Volkswagen.
Scenario: Pre-emptive Buffer Stock Build. $11,400
APPROVE — Pre-emptive Buffer Stock Build of 6,400 units to secure supply runway. If shipping delays exceed 14 days, initiate secondary plan: Emergency Airfreight.

title: Emergency Airfreight — SEMI-MCU-32
situation: SEMI-MCU-32 inventory depletion risk due to Suez disruption.
recommendation: APPROVE — Emergency Airfreight for 6,400 units to bypass 16-day shipping disruption and prevent $1.36M in SLA penalties.

Scenario: Emergency Airfreight. $271,000

title: Emergency Mitigation: Pre-emptive Buffer Stock Build — SEMI-MCU-32
situation: Critical inventory risk identified due to shipping lane disruption. Current runway: 12 days. Potential stockout by Day 12. Total exposure: $1.83M.
recommendation: APPROVE — Immediately increase safety stock to 60 days by pulling forward orders for SEMI-MCU-32. This provides the highest service level protection at the lowest cost, aligning with AutomotiveParts GmbH's low risk appetite.
